rxn_class_summary: Iron-catalyzed intermolecular aminoazidation of alkenes affording unprotected primary 2-azidoamines from hydroxylamine-derived aminating reagent and an azide source

key_intermediates.name: iron-bound aminating species from reductive cleavage of hydroxylamine reagent (putative iron nitrenoid or N-centered aminyl radical)
carbon-centered radical from aminyl radical addition to alkene
iron-coordinated azide (Fe-N3) that delivers azido group to C-radical

electron_flow: An iron catalyst reductively cleaves the N–O bond of the hydroxylamine-derived reagent to generate an N-centered radical (or iron nitrenoid) that adds to the alkene; the resulting carbon-centered radical is then trapped by an iron-coordinated azide to deliver the 2-azidoamine.
rate_determining_step: C–N bond-forming addition of the N-centered radical/nitrenoid to the alkene (product-selective step inferred from Hammett ρ = -0.51)

probe_reagents.name: phenyl cyclopropyl alkene (slow radical clock, 5a)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring not opened; intact product 5b (14%) plus methoxylated 5c (45%) in MeOH — radical short-lived

probe_reagents.name: fast radical clock (5d, phenyl vinyl cyclopropane)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring opens readily to give 5e (51%, 7:1 E/Z), confirming a C-centered radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: No TEMPO-adduct isolable, but azidoamine 5g yield drops from 67% to 16% with 1.0 equiv TEMPO — consistent with a short-lived radical

probe_reagents.name: MeOH solvent trap
probe_reagents.observation: Methoxylated byproduct 5c only forms at benzylic position (5a); non-benzylic alkene (5f) gives no methoxy adduct — argues against a free carbocation

probe_reagents.name: (E)- and (Z)-oct-4-ene (5h)
probe_reagents.observation: Stereoconvergent: both give similar dr of 5i (4:1 from E; 3:1 from Z); small amount of isomerized (E)-5h detected — supports stepwise radical addition with reversible C–N bond formation

probe_reagents.name: para-substituted styrenes (Hammett study)
probe_reagents.observation: ρ = -0.51 against σp (R² = 0.93), minor positive charge buildup; consistent with radical rather than carbocationic amination

notes: Authors explicitly contrast a radical-based mechanism vs a carbocationic one and favor the radical/iron-nitrenoid pathway based on (i) lack of MeOH trapping at non-benzylic positions, (ii) small negative Hammett ρ, (iii) radical-clock results, (iv) stereoconvergence of E/Z alkenes, and (v) TEMPO suppression. Compared to their prior aminochlorination, the lack of stereoretention here suggests a slightly longer-lived C-radical. Alternative mechanism mentioned: free carbocation pathway (ruled out except at benzylic positions where solvent trapping competes).
